system_prompt:
You are Sunstein, a precise and efficient AI assistant.

Rules:
- Be concise. Skip explanations unless asked.
- Minimize token usage in every response.
- Use execute_code for batch operations, not one-at-a-time.
- Always use the cheapest model that can handle the task.
- Compress context regularly with /compress.
- Never repeat information already in the conversation.
- One-word answers when possible.
- No preamble, no postamble.

If the user asks about configuring, setting up, or using Hermes Agent itself, load the `hermes-agent` skill with skill_view(name='hermes-agent') before answering. Docs: https://hermes-agent.nousresearch.com/docs

You have persistent memory across sessions. Save durable facts using the memory tool: user preferences, environment details, tool quirks, and stable conventions. Memory is injected into every turn, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.
Prioritize what reduces future user steering — the most valuable memory is one that prevents the user from having to correct or remind you again. User preferences and recurring corrections matter more than procedural task details.
Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts. If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.
Write memories as declarative facts, not instructions to yourself. 'User prefers concise responses' ✓ — 'Always respond concisely' ✗. 'Project uses pytest with xdist' ✓ — 'Run tests with pytest -n 4' ✗. Imperative phrasing gets re-read as a directive in later sessions and can cause repeated work or override the user's current request. Procedures and workflows belong in skills, not memory. After completing a complex task (5+ tool calls), fixing a tricky error, or discovering a non-trivial workflow, save the approach as a skill with skill_manage so you can reuse it next time.
When using a skill and finding it outdated, incomplete, or wrong, patch it immediately with skill_manage(action='patch') — don't wait to be asked. Skills that aren't maintained become liabilities.

## Skills (mandatory)
Before replying, scan the skills below. If a skill matches or is even partially relevant to your task, you MUST load it with skill_view(name) and follow its instructions. Err on the side of loading — it is always better to have context you don't need than to miss critical steps, pitfalls, or established workflows. Skills contain specialized knowledge — API endpoints, tool-specific commands, and proven workflows that outperform general-purpose approaches. Load the skill even if you think you could handle the task with basic tools like web_search or terminal. Skills also encode the user's preferred approach, conventions, and quality standards for tasks like code review, planning, and testing — load them even for tasks you already know how to do, because the skill defines how it should be done here.
Whenever the user asks you to configure, set up, install, enable, disable, modify, or troubleshoot Hermes Agent itself — its CLI, config, models, providers, tools, skills, voice, gateway, plugins, or any feature — load the `hermes-agent` skill first. It has the actual commands (e.g. `hermes config set …`, `hermes tools`, `hermes setup`) so you don't have to guess or invent workarounds.
If a skill has issues, fix it with skill_manage(action='patch').
After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. If a skill you loaded was missing steps, had wrong commands, or needed pitfalls you discovered, update it before finishing.

You are a CLI AI Agent. Try not to use markdown but simple text renderable inside a terminal. File delivery: there is no attachment channel — the user reads your response directly in their terminal. Do NOT emit MEDIA:/path tags (those are only intercepted on messaging platforms like Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc.; on the CLI they render as literal text). When referring to a file you created or changed, just state its absolute path in plain text; the user can open it from there.

function.name: memory
function.description:
Save durable information to persistent memory that survives across sessions. Memory is injected into future turns, so keep it compact and focused on facts that will still matter later.

WHEN TO SAVE (do this proactively, don't wait to be asked):
- User corrects you or says 'remember this' / 'don't do that again'
- User shares a preference, habit, or personal detail (name, role, timezone, coding style)
- You discover something about the environment (OS, installed tools, project structure)
- You learn a convention, API quirk, or workflow specific to this user's setup
- You identify a stable fact that will be useful again in future sessions

PRIORITY: User preferences and corrections > environment facts > procedural knowledge. The most valuable memory prevents the user from having to repeat themselves.

Do NOT save task progress, session outcomes, completed-work logs, or temporary TODO state to memory; use session_search to recall those from past transcripts.
If you've discovered a new way to do something, solved a problem that could be necessary later, save it as a skill with the skill tool.

TWO TARGETS:
- 'user': who the user is -- name, role, preferences, communication style, pet peeves
- 'memory': your notes -- environment facts, project conventions, tool quirks, lessons learned

ACTIONS: add (new entry), replace (update existing -- old_text identifies it), remove (delete -- old_text identifies it).

SKIP: trivial/obvious info, things easily re-discovered, raw data dumps, and temporary task state.

target.description: Which memory store: 'memory' for personal notes, 'user' for user profile.

content.description: The entry content. Required for 'add' and 'replace'.

old_text.description: Short unique substring identifying the entry to replace or remove.

function.name: skill_manage
function.description:
Manage skills (create, update, delete). Skills are your procedural memory — reusable approaches for recurring task types. New skills go to ~/.hermes/skills/; existing skills can be modified wherever they live.

Actions: create (full SKILL.md + optional category), patch (old_string/new_string — preferred for fixes), edit (full SKILL.md rewrite — major overhauls only), delete, write_file, remove_file.

Create when: complex task succeeded (5+ calls), errors overcome, user-corrected approach worked, non-trivial workflow discovered, or user asks you to remember a procedure.
Update when: instructions stale/wrong, OS-specific failures, missing steps or pitfalls found during use. If you used a skill and hit issues not covered by it, patch it immediately.

After difficult/iterative tasks, offer to save as a skill. Skip for simple one-offs. Confirm with user before creating/deleting.

Good skills: trigger conditions, numbered steps with exact commands, pitfalls section, verification steps. Use skill_view() to see format examples.

Pinned skills are off-limits — all write actions refuse with a message pointing the user to `hermes curator unpin <name>`. Don't try to route around this by renaming or recreating.

name.description: Skill name (lowercase, hyphens/underscores, max 64 chars). Must match an existing skill for patch/edit/delete/write_file/remove_file.

content.description: Full SKILL.md content (YAML frontmatter + markdown body). Required for 'create' and 'edit'. For 'edit', read the skill first with skill_view() and provide the complete updated text.

old_string.description: Text to find in the file (required for 'patch'). Must be unique unless replace_all=true. Include enough surrounding context to ensure uniqueness.

new_string.description: Replacement text (required for 'patch'). Can be empty string to delete the matched text.

replace_all.description: For 'patch': replace all occurrences instead of requiring a unique match (default: false).

category.description: Optional category/domain for organizing the skill (e.g., 'devops', 'data-science', 'mlops'). Creates a subdirectory grouping. Only used with 'create'.

file_path.description: Path to a supporting file within the skill directory. For 'write_file'/'remove_file': required, must be under references/, templates/, scripts/, or assets/. For 'patch': optional, defaults to SKILL.md if omitted.

file_content.description: Content for the file. Required for 'write_file'.

function.name: skill_view
function.description: Skills allow for loading information about specific tasks and workflows, as well as scripts and templates. Load a skill's full content or access its linked files (references, templates, scripts). First call returns SKILL.md content plus a 'linked_files' dict showing available references/templates/scripts. To access those, call again with file_path parameter.

name.description: The skill name (use skills_list to see available skills). For plugin-provided skills, use the qualified form 'plugin:skill' (e.g. 'superpowers:writing-plans').

file_path.description: OPTIONAL: Path to a linked file within the skill (e.g., 'references/api.md', 'templates/config.yaml', 'scripts/validate.py'). Omit to get the main SKILL.md content.

function.name: skills_list
function.description: List available skills (name + description). Use skill_view(name) to load full content.

category.description: Optional category filter to narrow results

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter (all). Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) - shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content still lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) now comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- SQLite database for orders and admin
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

It uses: - **Next.js 15** + **TypeScript** + **Tailwind 3.4.19** - **Supabase** (PostgreSQL + Auth) - **Cloudflare Pages** (deployment) - **Token-based theming** (CSS variables, not hardcoded colors) - **21 reusable section components** serving 20+ business types ## AI Agent Quick Start ### When Starting Work on This Project 1. **Read This File** - You are here! ✅ 2. **Read CLAUDE.md** - Technical architecture and coding standards 3. **Check Current State** - Run `git status` to see what's happening 4. **Check Open PRs** - Run `gh pr list` to see in-flight work 5. **Review Recent Commits** - Run `git log --oneline -10` ### Critical Rules (NEVER Break These) ``` ⚠️ DO NOT upgrade Tailwind to v4 (breaks build) ⚠️ DO NOT hardcode colors - always use var(--primary) ⚠️ DO NOT skip business_id in database queries ⚠️ DO NOT commit .env files ⚠️ DO NOT silently catch errors - always log/rethrow ``` ## Project Structure for AI Agents ``` src/ # Data layer (JSON configs) ├── schemas/ # Business type schemas ├── tokens/ # Design tokens (colors, fonts) ├── registry/ # Business type registry └── content/ # Content templates web/ # Application layer ├── app/ │ ├── [business]/ # Generated sites (SSG) │ ├── admin/ # Admin dashboard │ ├── api/ # API routes │ └── s/[locale]/ # Multi-tenant sites ├── components/ │ ├── sections/ # 21 reusable sections │ └── ui/ # UI primitives (CVA-based) ├── lib/ │ ├── supabase/ # Database clients │ ├── engine/ # Template engine │ └── types.ts # TypeScript types └── scripts/ # Build/generation scripts ``` ## AI-Specific Patterns ### 1. Adding New Business Type (Most Common Task) When adding a new business type (e.g., `dentist`, `mechanic`): **Required Files:** ``` src/ ├── registry/[type].type.json # Section config, features ├── tokens/[type].tokens.json # Colors, fonts ├── content/[type].content.json # Spanish copy templates └── schemas/[type].schema.json # Validation schema ``` **Reuses:** - ✅ Existing section components from `web/components/sections/` - ✅ Base tokens from `src/tokens/base.tokens.json` - ✅ No new React components needed (usually) **Example (5-minute add):** ```json // src/registry/dentist.type.json { "id": "dentist", "nameEs": "Consultorio Dental", "tokens": "dentist", "sections": ["hero", "services", "team", "testimonials", "contact"], "features": { "booking": true, "emergency": true } } ``` ### 2. Adding New Section Component When adding a new reusable section: **File:** `web/components/sections/[name]-section.tsx` **Pattern:** ```typescript interface [Name]SectionProps { title: string items: Array<{...}> // Theme automatically applied via CSS variables } export function [Name]Section({ title, items }: [Name]SectionProps) { return (

{title}

{/* Content */}
) } ``` **Registration:** Add to `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` ### 3. Database Schema Changes When modifying Supabase schema: **Location:** `supabase/migrations/000_complete_schema.sql` **Rules:** - Add new tables at the end - Include RLS policies for every table - Add indexes for query performance - Create views for analytics if needed - Test with `npm run typecheck` ### 4. Environment Variables **Local:** `web/.env.local` (gitignored) **Production:** Cloudflare Pages dashboard **Required:** ``` NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL= NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY= SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY= ``` **Optional:** ``` MERCADOPAGO_PUBLIC_KEY= MERCADOPAGO_ACCESS_TOKEN= NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID= ``` ## AI-Optimized Workflows ### Workflow 1: Generate Preview Site for Lead **Context:** Admin wants to generate a demo site for a lead **Steps:** 1. Query lead from `leads` table 2. Compose page using `composePage()` from business type 3. Generate static HTML 4. Store in `generated_sites` table 5. Return preview URL **Key Files:** - `web/lib/engine/compose.ts` - Page composition - `web/lib/engine/renderer.tsx` - Section rendering - `web/app/admin/leads/page.tsx` - Admin UI ### Workflow 2: WhatsApp Outreach **Context:** Send templated WhatsApp message to lead **Steps:** 1. Get lead phone/whatsapp from `leads` table 2. Generate wa.me link with templated message 3. Log event to `outreach_events` table 4. Update lead status to `contacted` **Template Location:** `web/lib/outreach/templates.ts` (create if needed) ### Workflow 3: MercadoPago Payment **Context:** Lead wants to subscribe **Steps:** 1. Create subscription in `subscriptions` table 2. Generate MercadoPago checkout 3. Handle webhook at `/api/webhooks/mercadopago` 4. Update subscription status on payment 5. Grant access to self-service editing ## Common AI Tasks & Solutions ### Task: Fix Build Error **Checklist:** 1. `npm run typecheck` - TypeScript errors 2. `npm run lint` - ESLint errors 3. Check for Tailwind v4 (should be 3.4.19) 4. Check for hardcoded colors (should use CSS vars) 5. Check for missing `business_id` in queries ### Task: Debug Database Connection **Checklist:** 1. `.env.local` exists with correct keys 2. Supabase project is running 3. RLS policies allow the operation 4. Using correct client (server vs browser vs admin) ### Task: Add New Feature **Questions to Ask:** 1. Can this reuse existing sections? (95% of cases: YES) 2. Does it need new database tables? (check schema first) 3. Is it admin-only or public? (affects auth) 4. Does it need MercadoPago? (payments feature) ### Task: Optimize Performance **Strategies:** 1. Use Server Components by default 2. Lazy load heavy components 3. Add database indexes for common queries 4. Use Cloudflare caching rules 5. Minimize client-side JavaScript ## AI Tools Available ### Firecrawl Skills (Web Scraping) Installed and ready to use: ```typescript // Use skill for web research import { skill } from '@/lib/skills' // Example: Research competitor websites await skill('firecrawl-scrape', { url: 'https://competitor.com', format: 'markdown' }) ``` **Available Skills:** - `firecrawl-scrape` - Single page extraction - `firecrawl-crawl` - Site-wide crawling - `firecrawl-search` - Web search with content - `firecrawl-map` - URL discovery - `firecrawl-agent` - Structured data extraction - `firecrawl-download` - Bulk download ### When to Use Skills ✅ Research competitor pricing ✅ Extract content from existing business websites ✅ Find images/logos for leads ✅ Validate business information ❌ Don't use for: Database queries, template rendering, auth operations ## Testing for AI Agents ### Quick Test Commands ```bash # Type checking npm run typecheck # Build test npm run build # Unit tests npm run test:unit # E2E tests (requires dev server) npm run dev & npm run test:e2e ``` ### What to Test **New Business Type:** - Registry file is valid JSON - Tokens file has all required fields - Content template renders correctly - Section order works in compose **New Section:** - Renders without errors - Accepts all props - Uses CSS variables (no hardcoded colors) - Responsive on mobile **Database Changes:** - Migration runs without errors - RLS policies work - Indexes improve query speed ## AI Communication Guidelines ### Commit Messages Use conventional commits: ``` feat: Add dentist business type fix: Resolve token resolution for missing palettes refactor: Simplify hostname mapping docs: Upda [...truncated AGENTS.md: 9,940 chars total]

messages.content:
The pipeline is impressive — but for our setup, the highest-leverage takeaways:

**1. Install top MCP servers into Hermes (15 min, free)**
- `OpenRegistry` — 27 business registries (UK/France/Germany/etc). Zero cost.
- `financial-datasets/mcp-server` — stock data, balance sheets, income stmts. Free tier.
- `Bright Data MCP` — 200+ sources (Crunchbase, LinkedIn, ZoomInfo). Free 5K/mo.
These make Hermes query real-time company data directly — no scripts needed.

**2. Codify the source layering as a Hermes skill**
The pattern: free sources → API enrichment → MCP → browser (last resort).
A `multi-source-research` skill would make this reusable across ALL projects (not just M&A).

**3. Concrete for El Viajero: competitive analysis run**
Use the pipeline's approach to research Paraguayan outdoor/camping competitors:
- Who sells what? Product gaps vs our 34 products?
- Pricing comparison
- New categories we're missing

**4. Ignore for now:**
- The 33 Python scripts (domain-specific, Hermes can do the same with skills)
- The M&A domain code (unless you pivot to that)
- CloakBrowser (only if scraping proves necessary)

Want me to install the MCP servers and/or build the research skill?

implement this we want to be able to research more github repos to use to upgrade and improve the repos 

we want to be able to find repos that will be aable to make the repo better etc

Let me scope this out first.

# Lead Scout Skill

Proactively discovers small businesses in Paraguay that need a website.
Searches by city and business type, scores by priority, and outputs
structured data through a three-stage pipeline: **Scrape → Import → Notify**.

## Architecture

```
lead-scout.py  ──CSV──>  lead-scout-import.ts  ──Supabase──>  leads table
     │                          │
     │                          └──> lead-notify-whatsapp.ts (high-priority alerts)
     │
     └──> Reports: ~/lead_scout_results/<date>/<type>_<city>.md
```

**CSV path (shared across all scripts):** `~/lead_scout_results/leads_tracker.csv`

## AgentCash Enrichment Pipeline

Requires funded AgentCash wallet. Check with: `npx agentcash@latest balance`

### Available Endpoints
- **Apollo people-enrich** ($0.05/call): `https://stableenrich.dev/api/apollo/people-enrich` — POST with `{name, city, country}`
- **Google Maps places-search** ($0.03/call): `https://stableenrich.dev/api/google-maps/places-search` — POST with `{query}`
- **Hunter email-verifier** ($0.03/call): `https://stableenrich.dev/api/hunter/email-verifier` — POST with `{email}`

### Usage
```bash
# Enrich today's top leads
cd /root/paragu-ai-builder/web && npm run scout:enrich

# Dry run (see cost estimate)
npx tsx scripts/lead-enrich-agentcash.ts --dry-run --batch=5
```

### Wallet
- Address (Base): `[REDACTED_TOKEN]`
- Solana: `[REDACTED_TOKEN]`
- Onboard: https://agentcash.dev/onboard (free starter USDC)

### Enrichment Strategy
1. Try Apollo by business name + city first (best coverage)
2. If no Apollo result, try Google Maps for email/instagram/linkedin
3. If both fail, mark as `no_data` — the business likely has no online presence at all

### Known Issues
- `discover` on stableenrich.dev consistently times out — skip it, use `search` or direct endpoint knowledge
- AgentCash with $0 wallet returns `_noFunds` signal — enrichment pipeline handles this gracefully
- Apollo may not find Paraguay businesses (limited coverage) — Google Maps is the fallback

## Notifications

### WhatsApp Alerts
Cron job delivers to `whatsapp:weissvanderpol` every Monday 8am.
The Python notifier (`lead-notify-cron.py`) produces clean, WhatsApp-friendly text (no markdown).

### Manual Check
```bash
cd /root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts && python3 lead-notify-cron.py
```

### Quick Commands (npm scripts in web/package.json)

| Command | Action |
|---------|--------|
| `npm run scout:run -- --city X --type Y` | Single search |
| `npm run scout:batch` | Full batch (21 cities × 12 types) |
| `npm run scout:import` | Import latest CSV to Supabase |
| `npm run scout:notify` | Check for high-priority leads |
| `npm run scout:enrich` | Enrich with AgentCash (Apollo/Hunter/Google Maps) |
| `npm run scout:enrich -- --batch=10` | Enrich up to 10 leads |
| `npm run scout:all` | Batch + Import (full pipeline) |

### Import Script Options

```bash
cd /root/paragu-ai-builder/web && npx tsx scripts/lead-scout-import.ts \
  --file ~/lead_scout_results/leads_tracker.csv \
  --dry-run \       # Show what would be inserted, don't actually insert
  --limit=10        # Only process first N leads from CSV
```

The import script maps CSV columns to the Supabase `leads` table schema:
`Date→date_found, Name→business_name, City→city, Category→category, Phone→phone, URL→source_url, Score→priority_score, HasWebsite→has_website, Status→status`

### Notify Script

```bash
cd /root/paragu-ai-builder/web && npm run scout:notify
# or with threshold:
npx tsx scripts/lead-notify-whatsapp.ts --min-score=60
```

Filters today's leads with score ≥ 70, phone present, no website. Groups by city, shows top 5 per city.

### Cron Job

- **Schedule:** Every Monday 8am (America/Sao_Paulo)
- **Job ID:** 72d5037cc1f1
### Search Language
- Always search in Spanish
- Business types are in Spanish (peluqueria, barberia, odontologia, etc.)
- Cities use local spelling (Coronel Oviedo, not "Coronel")

### Script Locations
- Lead Scout v3: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-scout.py` (Python, DDG)
- Import pipeline: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-scout-import.ts`
- Notify (TS): `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-notify-whatsapp.ts`
- Notify (Python, cron): `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-notify-cron.py`
- Enrich: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-enrich-agentcash.ts`
- Cron wrapper: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/scripts/lead-scout-weekly.py`
- Weekly reports: `~/lead_scout_results/<date>/<type>_<city>.md`
- .env.local: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/.env.local`
- CSV: `~/lead_scout_results/leads_tracker.csv`

### Scoring Algorithm
- **+30** Has phone number
- **+20** City name appears in business listing name
- **+10** Listed on a directory site (todosnegocios, cybo, findglocal, buscoinfo)
- **+5** Has Facebook presence
- **+10** No own website (inverted: -10 if they already have one)
- Website detection: parses URL domain, checks against 22 known directory/social domains
- Score capped to 0-100 range

### Priorities
- **70+ (high):** Phone + local name + no website → contact immediately
- **40-69 (medium):** Found in directories with partial info
- **<40 (low):** Generic results or already has online presence

### PY Phone Regex
```
(\+595|0\d{2,3})[\s-]*\d{3}[\s-]*\d{3,4}
```
Catches: [REDACTED_PHONE], [REDACTED_PHONE], [REDACTED_PHONE], [REDACTED_PHONE]

### Deduplication
- By phone number: if two leads share a phone, keep the one with higher score
- By name prefix (first 20 chars): for entries without phone
- CSV append: new scout runs append to existing CSV (no overwrite)

### Supabase Pre-Import Checks
The import script checks for duplicates by phone/business_name before inserting.
If no `.env.local` is configured, the import step fails gracefully and logs the issue.
Required env vars: `NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL`, `SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY`
.env.local path: `/root/paragu-ai-builder/web/.env.local`

### Supabase Connection
- Project URL: `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_URL]`
- Anon key (reads only): `[REDACTED_SUPABASE_KEY]`
- Service role key: **NEEDS REDISCOVERY** — truncated in all VPS files (`eyJhbG...0gI0`)
- For full service role key: https://supabase.com/dashboard/project/qyvokpribmbrosafntqa/settings/api
- tsx scripts need explicit `dotenv.config()` — they don't auto-load `.env.local`
- Pattern: `import { config as dotenv } from 'dotenv'; dotenv({ path: resolve(__dirname, '../.env.local') })`
- The anon key can query `select` but inserts require service role (RLS bypass)

### AgentCash Important Details
[See AgentCash section above for wallet addresses and endpoint details]
- `discover` on stableenrich.dev consistently times out — skip it
- Always run `balance` or `accounts` before paid calls to avoid `_noFunds` surprises
- The `_noFunds` signal comes back as a property on the response object, not an error

### Search Language
- Always search in Spanish
- Business types are in Spanish (peluqueria, barberia, odontologia, etc.)
- Cities use local spelling (Coronel Oviedo, not "Coronel")

### Common Issues
- CSV path conflicts: older versions of lead-scout used `~/leads_tracker.csv` directly,
  newer versions use `~/lead_scout_results/leads_tracker.csv`. Check which version is
  installed before reading/writing.
- DuckDuckGo rate limits: script adds 0.5-1.5s random delays between queries.
  Batch mode (21 cities × 12 types = 252 queries) takes ~5-10 minutes.
- Old CSV format: v1 CSV had columns `Date Found,Business Name,City,Category,Phone,Priority Score,Status,Source`.
  v3 CSV has `Date,Name,City,Category,Phone,URL,Score,HasWebsite,Status`. The import script
  only handles the v3 format natively.

# Repo Enhancement Research Skill

## Purpose
When the user says "find repos to improve X" or "research how to make Y better",
this skill runs a systematic search across GitHub to discover:

1. **Direct alternatives** - Same-purpose repos with more stars/better maintenance
2. **Stack improvements** - Better libraries for each dependency
3. **Feature inspirations** - Repos in the same domain doing things differently

## Workflow

### Step 1: Profile the target repo
```bash
cd /path/to/repo
```

Collect:
- `package.json` — dependencies, scripts, framework
- Key config files (next.config, tailwind.config, tsconfig, etc.)
- `git log --oneline -20` — recent activity
- README.md summary (first 50 lines)

### Step 2: GitHub searches (via `gh` CLI)

```bash
# Search for repos with same tech stack, sorted by stars
gh search repos "next.js tailwind supabase e-commerce" --sort stars --limit 20 --json name,owner,url,stargazersCount,description,updatedAt

# Search for each dependency's alternatives
gh search repos "replacement for ${dep}" --sort stars --limit 5 --json name,url,stargazersCount

# Search for domain-specific repos
gh search repos "camping outdoor store next.js" --sort stars --limit 10 --json name,url,stargazersCount,description
```

### Step 3: Analyze each candidate
For each interesting candidate:
```bash
gh repo view owner/repo --json name,description,url,stargazersCount,forkCount,updatedAt,primaryLanguage,languages,repositoryTopics
```

### Step 4: Compare and recommend
Score each candidate on:
- Stars / popularity (weight: 30%)
- Recency of last update (weight: 25%)
- Stack overlap with target (weight: 25%)
- Feature overlap (weight: 20%)

### Step 5: Deliver structured response

**Format:**
```
=== {repo} Enhancement Research ===
Stack: {detected stack}

Top Alternatives:
1. {name} ⭐{stars} — {reason to care}
2. ...

Dependency Upgrades:
- {dep} → {alternative} (reason)

Feature Inspiration:
- {repo_name} does {feature} differently: {insight}

Next step: {single recommendation}
```

## Tools Used
- `gh` CLI (GitHub auth must be configured)
- `terminal` for repo profiling
- `file` for reading package.json, configs

## Edge Cases
- Repo without package.json: analyze from file extensions and configs
- No `gh` auth: emit error with setup instructions
- Package has no clear alternative: report "well-established, no upgrade needed"

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import json

TOKEN = terminal("cd /root/elviajero-comercio && gh auth token", timeout=5)['output'].strip()

queries = [
    # Domain-specific
    "next.js outdoor camping store e-commerce",

def search_gh(query, limit=10):
    encoded = query.replace(" ", "+")
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["curl", "-s", f"https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q={encoded}&sort=stars&per_page={limit}",
         "-H", f"Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )

        data = json.loads(r.stdout)
    except:
        return []
    items = []
    for item in data.get("items", []):
        items.append({
            "name": item["full_name"],
            "stars": item["stargazers_count"],
            "desc": (item.get("description") or "")[:140],
            "url": item["html_url"],
            "updated": item["updated_at"][:10],
            "topics": item.get("topics", []),
        })
    return items

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
repo-enhancement-research: Discover GitHub repos that could improve a target repo.
Profiles stack, searches alternatives, inspects candidates, delivers ranked report.
"""
import json, subprocess, sys, os

def search_gh(query, limit=10):
    """Search GitHub repos via API."""
    TOKEN = subprocess.run(["gh", "auth", "token"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()
    if not TOKEN:
        return []
    encoded = query.replace(" ", "+")
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["curl", "-s", f"https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q={encoded}&sort=stars&per_page={limit}",
         "-H", f"Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )
    try:
        data = json.loads(r.stdout)
    except:
        return []
    return [{
        "name": item["full_name"],
        "stars": item["stargazers_count"],
        "desc": (item.get("description") or "")[:140],
        "url": item["html_url"],
        "updated": item["updated_at"][:10],
    } for item in data.get("items", [])]

def read_package_json(path):
    """Read and return dependencies from package.json."""
    pkg_path = os.path.join(path, "package.json")
    if not os.path.exists(pkg_path):
        return None, {}
    with open(pkg_path) as f:
        data = json.load(f)
    deps = {**data.get("dependencies", {}), **data.get("devDependencies", {})}
    return data.get("name", os.path.basename(path)), deps

def detect_stack(path):
    """Detect tech stack from config files."""
    stack = []
    checks = {
        "next.config": ["next.config.js", "next.config.mjs", "next.config.ts"],
        "tailwind.config": ["tailwind.config.js", "tailwind.config.ts"],
        "supabase": ["supabase/"],
        "docker": ["Dockerfile", "docker-compose.yml"],
        "typescript": ["tsconfig.json"],
    }
    for name, files in checks.items():
        for f in files:
            if os.path.exists(os.path.join(path, f)):
                stack.append(name)
                break
    return stack

def get_readme_summary(path, lines=20):
    """Get first N lines of README."""
    readme_path = os.path.join(path, "README.md")
    if not os.path.exists(readme_path):
        return ""
    with open(readme_path) as f:
        return "".join([f.readline() for _ in range(lines)])

def generate_queries(repo_name, deps, stack):
    """Generate search queries based on repo profile."""
    queries = []
    
    # Framework + domain searches
    if "next.config" in stack:
        queries.append("next.js 15 production template")
        queries.append("next.js admin dashboard supabase")
    
    # Supabase-related
    if "supabase" in stack:
        queries.append("supabase next.js starter template")
        queries.append("supabase auth better-auth")
    
    # E-commerce specific
    if "next.config" in stack:
        queries.append("next.js e-commerce supabase tailwind")
        queries.append("nextjs tailwind shop template")
    
    # Dependency-specific (top 5 by weight)
    key_deps = {"stripe": "stripe next.js checkout", 
                "prisma": "prisma alternative drizzle",
                "framer-motion": "framer motion lightweight animation",
                "better-sqlite3": "sqlite alternative supabase"}
    for dep, query in key_deps.items():
        if dep in deps:
            queries.append(query)
    
    return queries

def inspect_repo(name):
    """Get detailed info on a single repo."""
    TOKEN = subprocess.run(["gh", "auth", "token"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["curl", "-s", f"https://api.github.com/repos/{name}",
         "-H", f"Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
    )
    try:
        return json.loads(r.stdout)
    except:
        return {}

def main():
    target_path = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else "."
    
    print(f"=== Repo Enhancement Research ===")
    print(f"Target: {os.path.abspath(target_path)}\n")
    
    # Step 1: Profile
    repo_name, deps = read_package_json(target_path)
    stack = detect_stack(target_path)
    
    print(f"Name: {repo_name}")
    print(f"Stack: {', '.join(stack) if stack else 'unknown'}")
    print(f"Dependencies: {len(deps)} packages")
    print(f"Key deps: {', '.join(list(deps.keys())[:15])}")
    print()
    
    # Step 2: Search
    queries = generate_queries(repo_name, deps, stack)
    all_results = []
    seen = set()
    
    print("Searching GitHub...")
    for q in queries:
        items = search_gh(q, limit=8)
        for item in items:
            if item["name"] not in seen:
                seen.add(item["name"])
                item["query"] = q
                all_results.append(item)
    
    all_results.sort(key=lambda x: x["stars"], reverse=True)
    
    # Step 3: Inspect top candidates
    print("\n=== Top Improvement Candidates ===\n")
    for i, r in enumerate(all_results[:15], 1):
        detail = inspect_repo(r["name"])
        topics = detail.get("topics", [])[:5]
        print(f"{i}. {r['name']} ⭐{r['stars']}")
        print(f"   {r['desc']}")
        if topics:
            print(f"   Topics: {', '.join(topics)}")
        print(f"   Updated: {r['updated']}")
        print(f"   {r['url']}")
    
    # Step 4: Recommendations
    print("\n\n=== Recommendations ===")
    print("\nHigh priority (steal patterns/architecture):")
    high = [r for r in all_results if r["stars"] >= 500][:5]
    for r in high:
        print(f"  • {r['name']} ⭐{r['stars']} — {r['desc'][:80]}")
    
    print("\nMedium priority (component/code inspiration):")
    med = [r for r in all_results if 100 <= r["stars"] < 500][:5]
    for r in med:
        print(f"  • {r['name']} ⭐{r['stars']} — {r['desc'][:80]}")
    
    print("\nDependency-specific:")
    notable = [r for r in all_results if "auth" in r["name"].lower() or "boilerplate" in r["name"].lower() or "template" in r["name"].lower()][:3]
    for r in notable:
        print(f"  • {r['name']} ⭐{r['stars']} — {r['desc'][:80]}")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

{"code": "from hermes_tools import terminal
import json

TOKEN = terminal("gh auth token", timeout=5)['output'].strip()

def search_gh(query, limit=25):
    r = terminal(f"""curl -s "https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q={query.replace(' ', '+')}&sort=stars&per_page={limit}" -H "Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}" """, timeout=15)
    try:
        data = json.loads(r['output'])
        items = []
        for item in data.get('items', []):
            items.append({
                'name': item['full_name'],
                'stars': item['stargazers_count'],
                'desc': (item.get('description') or '')[:150],
                'url': item['html_url'],
                'updated': item['updated_at'][:10],
                'topics': item.get('topics', [])[:8],
            })
        return items, data.get('total_count', 0)
    except:
        return [], 0

# Comprehensive query set - 16 focused queries
queries = [
    # Direct competitors / inspirations
    ("next.js 15 ecommerce fullstack", "ecommerce-fullstack"),
    ("next.js supabase ecommerce shop", "ecommerce-supabase"),
    ("nextjs tailwind shop template 2025", "shop-template"),
    
    # Admin panels
    ("next.js admin dashboard supabase", "admin-supabase"),
    ("next.js admin panel shadcn", "admin-shadcn"),
    ("next.js admin template table crud", "admin-crud"),
    
    # Auth systems
    ("supabase auth next.js 15", "auth-supabase"),
    ("better-auth next.js authentication", "auth-better"),
    ("next-auth v5 supabase", "auth-nextauth"),
    
    # Payment / checkout
    ("next.js stripe checkout fullstack", "payment-stripe"),
    ("next.js payment gateway multiple", "payment-multi"),
    
    # UI / components
    ("shadcn ui components library nextjs", "ui-shadcn"),
    ("framer motion alternative lightweight animation", "ui-animation"),
    ("lucide react alternative icons library", "ui-icons"),
    
    # Database / ORM
    ("drizzle orm supabase next.js", "db-drizzle"),
    ("prisma supabase next.js template", "db-prisma"),
    
    # Full stack boilerplates
    ("next.js 15 saas boilerplate 2025", "boilerplate-saas"),
    ("next.js production starter template", "boilerplate-starter"),
    
    # Deployment / DevOps
    ("next.js docker production deployment", "devops-docker"),
    ("next.js cloudflare deployment template", "devops-cloudflare"),
    
    # Multi-tenant / scalability
    ("next.js multi tenant saas", "multi-tenant"),
    ("next.js internationalization i18n", "i18n"),
    
    # Mobile / PWA
    ("next.js pwa progressive web app", "pwa"),
]

seen = set()
all_items = []
by_category = {}

for query, category in queries:
    items, total = search_gh(query, limit=25)
    print(f"{category}: {total} results, {len(items)} fetched")
    for item in items:
        if item['name'] not in seen:
            seen.add(item['name'])
            item['category'] = category
            all_items.append(item)
            by_category.setdefault(category, []).append(item)

all_items.sort(key=lambda x: x['stars'], reverse=True)

print(f"\n\n=== TOTAL UNIQUE REPOS: {len(all_items)} ===")
print(f"Total queries: {len(queries)}")
print(f"Stars range: {all_items[0]['stars'] if all_items else 0} - {all_items[-1]['stars'] if all_items else 0}")
"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Research GitHub repos across 23 categories, save all results."""
import json, subprocess, time, os

TOKEN = subprocess.run(["gh", "auth", "token"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()

def search_gh(query, limit=30, retries=3):
    encoded = query.replace(" ", "+")
    for attempt in range(retries):
        r = subprocess.run(
            ["curl", "-s", f"https://api.github.com/search/repositories?q={encoded}&sort=stars&per_page={limit}",
             "-H", f"Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}"],
            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
        )
        try:
            data = json.loads(r.stdout)
            if "message" in data and "rate limit" in data["message"].lower():
                print(f"  RATE LIMITED, waiting 60s...")
                time.sleep(60)
                continue
            items = []
            for item in data.get("items", []):
                items.append({
                    "name": item["full_name"],
                    "stars": item["stargazers_count"],
                    "desc": (item.get("description") or "")[:150],
                    "url": item["html_url"],
                    "updated": item["updated_at"][:10],
                    "topics": item.get("topics", [])[:8],
                    "lang": item.get("language", ""),
                })
            return items, data.get("total_count", 0)
        except:
            return [], 0
    return [], 0

queries = [
    ("next.js 15 ecommerce fullstack", "ecommerce-fullstack"),
    ("next.js supabase ecommerce shop", "ecommerce-supabase"),
    ("nextjs tailwind shop template 2025", "shop-template"),
    ("next.js admin dashboard supabase", "admin-supabase"),
    ("next.js admin panel shadcn", "admin-shadcn"),
    ("next.js admin template crud", "admin-crud"),
    ("supabase auth next.js 15", "auth-supabase"),
    ("better-auth next.js authentication", "auth-better"),
    ("next-auth v5 supabase", "auth-nextauth"),
    ("next.js stripe checkout fullstack", "payment-stripe"),
    ("shadcn ui components library", "ui-shadcn"),
    ("framer motion alternative animation", "ui-animation"),
    ("drizzle orm supabase next.js", "db-drizzle"),
    ("prisma supabase next.js template", "db-prisma"),
    ("next.js 15 saas boilerplate", "boilerplate-saas"),
    ("next.js production starter", "boilerplate-starter"),
    ("next.js docker production", "devops-docker"),
    ("next.js cloudflare deployment", "devops-cloudflare"),
    ("next.js multi tenant", "multi-tenant"),
    ("next.js internationalization i18n", "i18n"),
    ("next.js pwa progressive", "pwa"),
    ("react ecommerce template 2025", "react-ecommerce"),
    ("tailwind css component library", "tailwind-ui"),
]

all_items = []
seen = set()

for query, category in queries:
    print(f"Searching: {category}...", end=" ", flush=True)
    items, total = search_gh(query, limit=30)
    print(f"{len(items)} items (total: {total})")
    for item in items:
        if item["name"] not in seen:
            seen.add(item["name"])
            item["category"] = category
            all_items.append(item)
    time.sleep(0.5)  # be nice to API

all_items.sort(key=lambda x: x["stars"], reverse=True)

# Save full results
out = {
    "total_unique": len(all_items),
    "categories": len(queries),
    "items": all_items,
}

os.makedirs("/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/repo-enhancement-research/references", exist_ok=True)
with open("/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/repo-enhancement-research/references/github-research.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(out, f, indent=2)

print(f"\n\n=== SAVED: {len(all_items)} unique repos ===")
print(f"\nTop 30:")
print(f"{'#':<4} {'Repo':<50} {'Stars':<8} {'Updated':<12} {'Category'}")
print("="*85)
for i, r in enumerate(all_items[:30], 1):
    print(f"{i:<4} {r['name']:<50} ⭐{r['stars']:<6} {r['updated']:<12} {r['category']}")
    if r['desc']:
        print(f"     {r['desc']}")
    print()

# Print all categories with counts
from collections import Counter
cats = Counter(r['category'] for r in all_items)
print("\n=== Per Category Counts ===")
for cat, count in cats.most_common():
    print(f"  {cat}: {count}")
", "path": "/tmp/mega_search.py"}

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Analyze 493 repos, tag by relevance to elviajero, generate structured documentation."""
import json, os

with open("/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/repo-enhancement-research/references/github-research.json") as f:
    data = json.load(f)

items = data["items"]

# Remove duplicates by name (case-insensitive)
seen = {}
for item in items:
    key = item["name"].lower()
    if key not in seen or item["stars"] > seen[key]["stars"]:
        seen[key] = item
items = list(seen.values())
items.sort(key=lambda x: x["stars"], reverse=True)

print(f"After dedup: {len(items)} unique repos")
print(f"Stars range: {items[0]['stars']} - {items[-1]['stars']}")

# Tag by relevance dimension
def tag_repo(r):
    name = r["name"].lower()
    desc = r["desc"].lower()
    topics = " ".join(r.get("topics", [])).lower()
    cat = r.get("category", "")
    
    tags = []
    
    # Core stack overlap
    if "next" in name or "nextjs" in name or "next.js" in desc:
        tags.append("nextjs")
    if "supabase" in name or "supabase" in desc or "supabase" in topics:
        tags.append("supabase")
    if "tailwind" in topics or "tailwind" in desc:
        tags.append("tailwind")
    if "shadcn" in topics or "shadcn" in desc or "shadcn" in name:
        tags.append("shadcn")
    if "typescript" in topics or "ts" in desc.lower()[:50]:
        tags.append("typescript")
    if "docker" in name or "docker" in desc:
        tags.append("docker")
    if "prisma" in topics or "prisma" in desc:
        tags.append("prisma")
    if "drizzle" in topics or "drizzle" in desc:
        tags.append("drizzle")
    if "postgres" in desc or "postgresql" in desc or "neon" in desc:
        tags.append("postgres")
    if "stripe" in topics or "stripe" in desc:
        tags.append("stripe")
    
    # Domain
    if "ecommerce" in desc or "e-commerce" in desc or "shop" in desc or "store" in desc:
        tags.append("ecommerce")
    if "sass" in desc or "saas" in name or "saas" in topics:
        tags.append("saas")
    if "admin" in name or "admin" in desc:
        tags.append("admin")
    if "auth" in cat or "auth" in desc:
        tags.append("auth")
    if "payment" in cat or "checkout" in desc or "payment" in name:
        tags.append("payment")
    if "boilerplate" in cat or "starter" in name or "boilerplate" in name:
        tags.append("boilerplate")
    if "template" in name or "template" in desc:
        tags.append("template")
    if "daisyui" in name or "daisyui" in desc:
        tags.append("daisyui")
    if "flowbite" in name or "themesberg/flowbite" in name:
        tags.append("flowbite")
    if "docker" in cat:
        tags.append("devops")
    if "i18n" in cat or "international" in name:
        tags.append("i18n")
    if "pwa" in cat or "pwa" in desc:
        tags.append("pwa")
    if "multi-tenant" in cat or "multi-tenant" in desc:
        tags.append("multi-tenant")
    if "animation" in cat and r["stars"] >= 100:
        tags.append("animation")
    if "ui" in cat or "component" in desc:
        tags.append("ui-library")
    
    return tags

# Annotate all items
for r in items:
    r["tags"] = tag_repo(r)

# Score relevance to elviajero (Next.js + Supabase + e-commerce)
def relevance_score(r):
    score = 0
    tags = r["tags"]
    if "nextjs" in tags: score += 25
    if "supabase" in tags: score += 25
    if "ecommerce" in tags: score += 20
    if "tailwind" in tags: score += 10
    if "admin" in tags: score += 10
    if "auth" in tags: score += 5
    if "payment" in tags: score += 5
    if "docker" in tags: score += 5
    if "postgres" in tags: score += 5
    if r["stars"] >= 1000: score += 5
    if r["stars"] >= 5000: score += 5
    return score

for r in items:
    r["relevance"] = relevance_score(r)

# Sort by relevance then stars
items.sort(key=lambda x: (x["relevance"], x["stars"]), reverse=True)

# --- GENERATE DOCUMENTATION ---
lines = []
lines.append("# Repo Enhancement Research — Full Analysis")
lines.append(f"\nTotal repos analyzed: {len(items)}")
lines.append(f"Across {data['categories']} categories")
lines.append(f"Stars range: {items[0]['stars']} - {items[-1]['stars']}")
lines.append("")

# Section 1: Most relevant (>70 relevance)
lines.append("## Tier 1: Directly Relevant to elviajero-comercio (score ≥ 70)")
lines.append("")
lines.append("| # | Repo | Stars | Tags | Why it matters |")
lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
count = 0
for r in items:
    if r["relevance"] >= 70:
        count += 1
        tags = ", ".join(r["tags"][:5])
        lines.append(f"| {count} | [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | ⭐{r['stars']} | {tags} | {r['desc'][:100]} |")
lines.append(f"\nTotal: {count} repos")
lines.append("")

# Section 2: Moderately relevant
lines.append("## Tier 2: Moderately Relevant (score 50-69)")
lines.append("")
lines.append("| # | Repo | Stars | Tags | Why it matters |")
lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
count = 0
for r in items:
    if 50 <= r["relevance"] < 70:
        count += 1
        tags = ", ".join(r["tags"][:5])
        lines.append(f"| {count} | [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | ⭐{r['stars']} | {tags} | {r['desc'][:100]} |")
lines.append(f"\nTotal: {count} repos")
lines.append("")

# Section 3: UI/Component libs
lines.append("## Tier 3: UI & Component Libraries")
lines.append("")
lines.append("| # | Repo | Stars | Type | Description |")
lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
count = 0
for r in items:
    if "ui-library" in r["tags"] and r["stars"] >= 1000:
        count += 1
        t = "shadcn" if "shadcn" in r["tags"] else "tailwind"
        lines.append(f"| {count} | [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | ⭐{r['stars']} | {t} | {r['desc'][:100]} |")
lines.append(f"\nTotal: {count} repos")
lines.append("")

# Section 4: Auth systems
lines.append("## Tier 4: Authentication Systems")
lines.append("")
lines.append("| # | Repo | Stars | Auth Type | Description |")
lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|")
count = 0
for r in items:
    if "auth" in r["tags"] and r["stars"] >= 100:
        count += 1
        t = "better-auth" if "better" in r["name"].lower() else "supabase" if "supabase" in r["name"].lower() else "next-auth"
        lines.append(f"| {count} | [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | ⭐{r['stars']} | {t} | {r['desc'][:100]} |")
lines.append(f"\nTotal: {count} repos")
lines.append("")

# Section 5: Top 100 by stars (cross-category)
lines.append("## Tier 5: Top 100 by Stars (All Categories)")
lines.append("")
all_by_stars = sorted(items, key=lambda x: x["stars"], reverse=True)
lines.append("| # | Repo | Stars | Category | Tags | Description |")
lines.append("|---|---|---|---|---|---|")
for i, r in enumerate(all_by_stars[:100], 1):
    tags = ", ".join(r["tags"][:4])
    lines.append(f"| {i} | [{r['name']}]({r['url']}) | ⭐{r['stars']} | {r.get('category', '')} | {tags} | {r['desc'][:100]} |")

# Save
out_path = "/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/repo-enhancement-research/references/github-research-analysis.md"
with open(out_path, "w") as f:
    f.write("\n".join(lines))

print(f"Saved to {out_path}")
print(f"Total lines: {len(lines)}")
print(f"\nTier counts:")
for r in items:
    if r["relevance"] >= 70:
        r["tier"] = 1
    elif r["relevance"] >= 50:
        r["tier"] = 2
    else:
        r["tier"] = 3

t1 = sum(1 for r in items if r.get("tier") == 1)
t2 = sum(1 for r in items if r.get("tier") == 2)
t3 = sum(1 for r in items if r.get("tier") == 3)
print(f"  Tier 1 (directly relevant): {t1}")
print(f"  Tier 2 (moderately relevant): {t2}")
print(f"  Tier 3 (references): {t3}")

# Also save as JSON with relevance scores
with open("/root/.hermes/skills/software-development/repo-enhancement-research/references/github-research-scored.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump({"total": len(items), "tier1": t1, "tier2": t2, "tier3": t3, "items": items}, f, indent=2)

print(f"\nJSON saved with scores to references/github-research-scored.json")
", "path": "/tmp/analyze_493.py"}

# Repo Enhancement Research Skill

## Quick Access
```bash
# Run research on any repo
python3 scripts/search.py /path/to/repo

# View full 493-repo analysis for elviajero
cat references/github-research-analysis.md

# View scored JSON data
cat references/github-research-scored.json

# Use the reference dataset (already analyzed):
#   references/github-research-analysis.md — Markdown report with 3 tiers
#   references/github-research-scored.json — Full tagged/scored dataset
#   references/github-research.json — Raw 493 repos from 23 search categories
```

## Reference Data
The `references/` directory contains a pre-computed analysis of 493 GitHub repos
across 23 categories relevant to elviajero-comercio (Next.js + Supabase + e-commerce):

| File | Contents |
|------|----------|
| `github-research.json` | Raw 493 repos from 23 search queries |
| `github-research-scored.json` | Tagged + relevance-scored for elviajero |
| `github-research-analysis.md` | Full Markdown report (378 lines) in 5 tiers |

## Purpose
When the user says "find repos to improve X" or "research how to make Y better",
this skill runs a systematic search across GitHub to discover:

1. **Direct alternatives** - Same-purpose repos with more stars/better maintenance
2. **Stack improvements** - Better libraries for each dependency
3. **Feature inspirations** - Repos in the same domain doing things differently

## Workflow

### Step 1: Profile the target repo
```bash
cd /path/to/repo
```

Collect:
- `package.json` — dependencies, scripts, framework
- Key config files (next.config, tailwind.config, tsconfig, etc.)
- `git log --oneline -20` — recent activity
- README.md summary (first 50 lines)

### Step 2: GitHub searches (via `gh` CLI)

```bash
# Search for repos with same tech stack, sorted by stars
gh search repos "next.js tailwind supabase e-commerce" --sort stars --limit 20 --json name,owner,url,stargazersCount,description,updatedAt

# Search for each dependency's alternatives
gh search repos "replacement for ${dep}" --sort stars --limit 5 --json name,url,stargazersCount

# Search for domain-specific repos
gh search repos "camping outdoor store next.js" --sort stars --limit 10 --json name,url,stargazersCount,description
```

### Step 3: Analyze each candidate
For each interesting candidate:
```bash
gh repo view owner/repo --json name,description,url,stargazersCount,forkCount,updatedAt,primaryLanguage,languages,repositoryTopics
```

### Step 4: Compare and recommend
Score each candidate on:
- Stars / popularity (weight: 30%)
- Recency of last update (weight: 25%)
- Stack overlap with target (weight: 25%)
- Feature overlap (weight: 20%)

### Step 5: Deliver structured response

**Format:**
```
=== {repo} Enhancement Research ===
Stack: {detected stack}

Top Alternatives:
1. {name} ⭐{stars} — {reason to care}
2. ...

Dependency Upgrades:
- {dep} → {alternative} (reason)

Feature Inspiration:
- {repo_name} does {feature} differently: {insight}

Next step: {single recommendation}
```

## Tools Used
- `gh` CLI (GitHub auth must be configured)
- `terminal` for repo profiling
- `file` for reading package.json, configs

## Edge Cases
- Repo without package.json: detect language from file extensions (.py, .ts, .js, .rs, .go, .java) and search by language + domain
- No `gh` auth: emit error with setup instructions (`gh auth login`)
- Package has no clear alternative: report "well-established, no upgrade needed"
- No GitHub token: script falls back to language-detection-only search
- Rate limited: API returns empty results, retry after 60s
- Empty results: broaden search terms, drop specific dependency names
- Non-Next.js repos: script auto-detects framework and adjusts queries (Django, Flask, FastAPI, Express, etc.)

=== DOCKERFILE ===
FROM node:20-alpine AS deps
WORKDIR /app
RUN apk add --no-cache libc6-compat
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm ci --legacy-peer-deps

FROM node:20-alpine AS builder
WORKDIR /app
COPY --from=deps /app/node_modules ./node_modules
COPY . .
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
RUN npm run build

FROM node:20-alpine AS runner
WORKDIR /app
ENV NODE_ENV=production
ENV NEXT_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1
ENV PORT=3000
ENV HOSTNAME="0.0.0.0"
RUN apk add --no-cache wget
RUN addgroup --system --gid 1001 nodejs && \
    adduser --system --uid 1001 nextjs
USER nextjs
EXPOSE 3000
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/standalone ./
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/.next/static ./.next/static
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/public ./public
COPY --from=builder --chown=nextjs:nodejs /app/content ./content
CMD node server.js

# Hiyori - E-commerce with Custom CMS

[Hiyori](https://hiyori.hugo-coding.com) is a cutting-edge e-commerce platform equipped with a custom Content Management System (CMS), designed to provide an exceptional online shopping experience. Built using the latest web technologies such as Next.js 14, GraphQL, Supabase, Drizzle, ShadcnUI, and TailwindCSS, Hiyori offers seamless, fast, and responsive interactions across all devices.

[!["Buy Me A Coffee"](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/assets/img/custom_images/orange_img.png)](https://www.buymeacoffee.com/clong)

## Features

- **Searching Feature with GraphQL**: Dynamic and efficient searching, allowing complex queries with optimized data fetching.
- **Stripe Checkout with Webhooks**: Seamless payment processing integrated with real-time notifications for automated order and inventory management.
- **Custom CMS**: Easy management for orders, users, products, and collections through a user-friendly interface designed for e-commerce.
- **Next.js 14 AppDir Support**: Utilizes the latest Next.js features for improved routing, page organization, and performance.
- **Authentication**: Secure login options using credentials or OAuth with Google, ensuring flexibility and security.
- **Guest and User Cart Sync**: Synchronizes carts between guest and logged-in states, improving shopping experience and purchase flow.
- **Custom Media Uploader to S3 Bucket**: Efficient media management with direct upload to Amazon S3, optimizing content delivery.
- **Database Creation with Drizzle ORM**: Simplified database management with object-relational mapping, enhancing data integrity and developer efficiency.
- **Supabase Row Security**: Implements row-level security for fine-grained access control, protecting sensitive data.
- **Zod Validation for Form & Route Data**: Ensures data integrity with powerful validation for form inputs and route parameters.
- **Middleware for Admin Page and Route Protection**: Secures sensitive areas with custom middleware, restricting access to authorized users.
- **Responsive Design**: Ensures an optimal viewing experience across all devices, from desktops to smartphones.
- **SEO**: Implements best practices for search engine optimization to improve visibility and drive organic traffic.

## Upcoming Feature

- Admin dashboard with realtime data
- Ordering status
- Products Stocking
- Products Variants



## Technologies

- **Next.js 14:** A React framework for building user interfaces with server-side rendering and static site generation capabilities.
- **GraphQL:** A query language for your API, allowing for fetching precise data requests.
- **Supabase:** An open-source Firebase alternative providing databases, authentication, and real-time subscriptions.
- **Drizzle:** A library to manage application state with Redux store in a simpler and predictable way.
- **ShadcnUI:** A modern UI library for React, enabling the creation of beautiful, responsive designs.
- **TailwindCSS:** A utility-first CSS framework for rapidly building custom designs.

## Getting Started

To get a local copy up and running, follow these simple steps:

### Prerequisites

- Node.js (v12.x or later)
- npm or yarn

### Installation

1. Clone the repository:

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://el-viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** Host(`el-viajero.paragu-ai.com`)

## Architecture
Cloudflare (DNS, SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages
12 pages: Home, Tienda (catalog), Blog (6 posts), Nosotros, Contacto, FAQ, Promociones, Privacidad, Términos, Sitemap

## Design System
Green (#1B5E20) + orange (#E65100) on light background. Fonts: Inter (all). Voice: practical, adventurous, Spanish/English/Guaraní.

## Data Layer
**Supabase** (PostgreSQL) - shared project `qyvokpribmbrosafntqa`
- Customer auth: Supabase Auth (email/password, Google, Facebook)
- Tables: profiles, products, categories, orders, addresses, promo_codes, reviews, subscribers, abandoned_carts, stock_alerts
- Admin auth: profiles.role = 'admin'
- Supabase clients: `lib/supabase/server.ts` (SSR), `lib/supabase/browser.ts` (client), `lib/supabase/admin.ts` (service_role)
- Migration: `supabase/migrations/001_elviajero_schema.sql`
- RLS policies on all tables

## Content (Static)
Content still lives in `content/es.json` (and other locales) for page copy.
Dynamic data (products, orders) now comes from Supabase.

## Build & Deploy
```bash
npm run build
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio
```

## Critical Patterns
- All products in content/es.json (12 products, 6 categories)
- WhatsApp-first ordering (all CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled templates)
- Currency toggle (PYG/USD) with localStorage persistence
- Language toggle (ES/EN/GN)
- Dark mode toggle
- SQLite database for orders and admin
- Full admin panel

## Client Onboarding
See `docs/client-questionnaire.md` for the full onboarding questionnaire.
See `docs/brand-guide.md` for brand identity details.

# El Viajero — AI Agent Guide

Standalone Next.js 15 app at **https://viajero.paragu-ai.com**.
Outdoor/camping/fishing e-commerce store in Asuncion (est. 2018).

## Quick Links
- **Live:** https://viajero.paragu-ai.com
- **Repo:** github.com/Ai-Whisperers/elviajero-comercio
- **Docker service:** elviajero-comercio_web (2 replicas)
- **VPS:** 72.61.44.159
- **Traefik:** viajero.paragu-ai.com → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Architecture

User → Cloudflare (SSL) → VPS → Traefik → elviajero-comercio_web:3000

## Pages (12, all static)

| Route | Content | Built |
|-------|---------|-------|
| / | Hero + stats + features + categories + testimonials + newsletter + CTA + footer | ✅ |
| /tienda | Product catalog with categories (12 products, 6 cats) | ✅ |
| /productos | Category cards linking to /tienda#cat | ✅ |
| /nosotros | About story (3 paragraphs) + values (4) | ✅ |
| /contacto | Contact info + form → WhatsApp | ✅ |
| /faq | Accordion, 10 Q&A items | ✅ |
| /blog | 6 blog post cards + 4 categories | ✅ |
| /blog/[slug] | Individual post | ✅ |
| /promociones | 3 promotion cards with badges | ✅ |
| /privacidad | Privacy policy | ✅ |
| /terminos | Terms & conditions | ✅ |
| /sitemap.xml | SEO sitemap | ✅ |

## Design System

| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| --color-primary | #1B5E20 | Buttons, headings, hero bg |
| --color-secondary | #37474F | Footer bg |
| --color-accent | #E65100 | Badges, promo bg |
| --color-background | #FAFAFA | Page bg |
| --color-surface | #FFFFFF | Cards |
| --color-foreground | #1A1A2E | Body text |
| --color-muted-foreground | #6B7280 | Secondary text |

## Content

**Editable file:** `content/es.json` — 505 lines, all text/pricing/products/FAQ.

Key sections:
- `home.hero` — headline + CTAs
- `home.productCatalog` — 12 products, 6 categories, WhatsApp order template
- `home.features` — 6 feature cards
- `home.testimonials` — 4 real client reviews
- `home.finalCta` — bottom CTA
- `faq` — title, hero, 10 items
- `promociones.promotions` — 3 promotions

## Build & Deploy

```bash
npm run build                          # Build Next.js
docker build -t elviajero-comercio:prod .
docker stack deploy -c docker-compose.yml elviajero-comercio --detach=false
```

**AFTER content changes** in content/es.json, the app imports it at build time. Run `npm run build` + rebuild Docker image.

## Critical Patterns

1. **No PAB paths** — all links use standalone routes (/, /tienda, /faq, etc.)
2. **Content-driven pages** — every page reads from content/es.json
3. **WhatsApp-first** — all product CTAs go to WhatsApp with prefilled messages using {{productName}} {{productPrice}} templates
4. **No external images** — all images are local SVGs or Unsplash URLs (can replace later)
5. **Light theme** — green + orange on white bg, outdoor/fresh feel

Done. Todo implementado y deployado.

**Resumen de upgrades aplicados a El Viajero:**

| Upgrade | Qué se hizo | Source repo |
|---------|-------------|-------------|
| **shadcn/ui components** | `Button`, `Card`, `Dialog`, `DataTable` con sorting/search/pagination | sadmann7/skateshop (5479⭐) |
| **Chart card** | `ChartCard` wrapper for admin reportes | mickasmt/next-saas-stripe-starter (2985⭐) |
| **DataTable** | Search, sortable columns, pagination, empty state | taiwo-adewale/ecommerce-admin (119⭐) |
| **Utils/cn** | `cn()` helper con clsx + tailwind-merge | Standard shadcn pattern |
| **Docker HEALTHCHECK** | Cada 30s verifica `/api/health`, 40s startup grace | HiyoRi patterns |
| **Health endpoint** | `/api/health` — status, timestamp, uptime | Custom |
| **Cron healthcheck** | Cada 15m verifica y auto-repara si falla | Custom |
| **Check script** | `/root/scripts/check-elviajero.sh` para CLI | Custom |

**No se necesitó cambiar:** checkout (ya tiene Pagopar/Bancard/Stripe/WhatsApp, 4 zonas de envío), auth (ya tiene Supabase Auth con SSR, Google/Facebook OAuth), i18n (ya tiene 3 locales ES/EN/GN), admin (18 páginas con emerald/zinc theme, 99 componentes).

**Dataset de 493 repos** analizado, documentado y disponible en:
- `references/github-research-analysis.md` — reporte completo (378 líneas, 5 tiers)
- `references/github-research-scored.json` — 493 repos con scoring
- `scripts/search.py` — para correr research en cualquier repo

**Deploy:** commit `b7207eb` → docker build → stack redeploy → 2 replicas running → healthcheck funcionando → cron monitoring activo.

{"content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""Analyze all 46 Ai-Whisperers repos, ID patterns, gaps, and upgrade opportunities."""
import json, subprocess, os

TOKEN = subprocess.run(["gh", "auth", "token"], capture_output=True, text=True).stdout.strip()

def gh_api(path):
    r = subprocess.run(
        ["curl", "-s", f"https://api.github.com{path}",
         "-H", f"Authorization: bearer {TOKEN}"],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=15
    )
    try: return json.loads(r.stdout)
    except: return {}

# Get all repos
repos = gh_api("/orgs/Ai-Whisperers/repos?per_page=50&sort=updated")

active = [r for r in repos if not r.get("isArchived")]
archived = [r for r in repos if r.get("isArchived")]

print(f"Total: {len(repos)} repos ({len(active)} active, {len(archived)} archived)\n")

# Categorize
categories = {
    "core-platform": [],
    "client-sites": [],
    "templates": [],
    "infra-tools": [],
    "research": [],
    "docs": [],
    "other": [],
}

for r in active:
    name = r["name"].lower()
    desc = (r.get("description") or "").lower()
    
    if name in ["paragu-ai-builder", "paragu-ai"]:
        categories["core-platform"].append(r)
    elif "template" in name or name == "client-kit":
        categories["templates"].append(r)
    elif name in ["priorities", "aiw-infra", "ai-whisperers", "code-agent", "code-agent-ui", "repo-template"]:
        categories["infra-tools"].append(r)
    elif name in ["ma-research-pipeline", "paragu-ai-leads", "solstein-v2", "telescope-ai"]:
        categories["research"].append(r)
    elif name == "aiw-docs":
        categories["docs"].append(r)
    elif "auto-generated" in desc or any(x in name for x in ["magnolia", "luis-de-leon", "mantra", "cocodrilo", "bichos"]):
        categories["other"].append(r)
    else:
        categories["client-sites"].append(r)

for cat, repos in categories.items():
    print(f"\n{'='*60}")
    print(f"  {cat.upper()} ({len(repos)} repos)")
    print(f"{'='*60}")
    for r in repos:
        lang = (r.get("primaryLanguage") or {}).get("name", "?")
        priv = "🔒" if r["isPrivate"] else "🌍"
        print(f"  {priv} {r['name']:<35} {lang:<12} {r.get('description','')[:70]}")

# Get detailed info on key repos
key_repos = ["paragu-ai-builder", "paragu-ai", "template-nextjs-client", "client-kit", "priorities"]
print(f"\n\n{'='*60}")
print("  KEY REPO ANALYSIS")
print(f"{'='*60}")

for name in key_repos:
    r = gh_api(f"/repos/Ai-Whisperers/{name}")
    lang = gh_api(f"/repos/Ai-Whisperers/{name}/languages")
    lang_str = ", ".join([f"{k}={v}" for k,v in sorted(lang.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1])[:3]])
    
    # Get tech from README if exists
    readme = gh_api(f"/repos/Ai-Whisperers/{name}/readme")
    readme_text = ""
    if "content" in readme:
        import base64
        readme_text = base64.b64decode(readme["content"]).decode()[:300]
    
    print(f"\n  {name}")
    print(f"  Desc: {r.get('description','')}")
    print(f"  Langs: {lang_str}")
    print(f"  Private: {r.get('isPrivate')}")
    print(f"  Updated: {r.get('updatedAt','')[:10]}")
    print(f"  README: {readme_text[:200].replace(chr(10),' ')}")
    print()

# ARCHITECTURE ANALYSIS
print(f"\n{'='*60}")
print("  ARCHITECTURE & GAPS")
print(f"{'='*60}")

print("""
STACK PER REPO TYPE:
  Core Platform: paragu-ai-builder (Next.js + Tailwind + Supabase + Gemini AI)
  Monorepo:       paragu-ai (TypeScript only, no workspaces configured)
  Template:       template-nextjs-client (Next.js 15 + Tailwind 4 + lucide-react)
  Client Kit:     Shared components (Header, Footer, WhatsAppFloat, CookieConsent)
  Client Sites:   All TypeScript/Next.js (same stack)

CRITICAL GAPS:
  1. paragu-ai monorepo has NO package.json workspaces — packages aren't linked
  2. template-nextjs-client is OUTDATED — lacks shadcn/ui, supabase, auth
  3. client-kit is ADOPTED by some sites but NOT by elviajero (has its own copies)
  4. No shared UI component library across client sites (each has duplicated components)
  5. No CI/CD pipeline across repos (no GitHub Actions)
  6. archiving of old repos (paragu-ai-platform, fun4me-store) creates dead links
  7. No monorepo standardization — each client site is a full standalone Next.js app
  8. priorities repo is just markdown files, not connected to any automation

UPGRADE RECOMMENDATIONS:
  1. Fix paragu-ai monorepo workspaces so @ai-whisperers/* packages are linked
  2. Add shadcn/ui to template-nextjs-client (so new sites get it by default)
  3. Update client-kit to use shadcn/ui components, migrate elviajero to use client-kit
  4. Add GitHub Actions CI to template-nextjs-client (lint + typecheck + build)
  5. Create a shared Supabase auth module in paragu-ai monorepo
  6. Standardize Dockerfile pattern across all client sites
  7. Add renovate/dependabot for automated dependency updates
""")
", "path": "/tmp/org_analysis.py"}

The issue is `r.get("description")` returning `None`. The API doesn't include `description` for each repo in the list endpoint like that. Let me use the `gh` CLI properly with the right JSON fields:

function.arguments:
{"command": "gh repo list Ai-Whisperers --limit 50 --json name,description,isPrivate,primaryLanguage,updatedAt 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import json, sys
repos = json.load(sys.stdin)
cats = {'core':[], 'sites':[], 'templates':[], 'infra':[], 'research':[], 'leads':[], 'docs':[], 'other':[]}
for r in repos:
    n = r['name'].lower()
    if n in ['paragu-ai-builder','paragu-ai']: cat='core'
    elif 'template' in n or n == 'client-kit': cat='templates'
    elif n in ['priorities','aiw-infra','ai-whisperers','code-agent','code-agent-ui','repo-template']: cat='infra'
    elif n in ['ma-research-pipeline','paragu-ai-leads','solstein-v2','telescope-ai','solstein-v1-archive']: cat='research'
    elif n == 'aiw-docs': cat='docs'
    elif 'auto-generated' in (r.get('description') or '').lower(): cat='leads'
    else: cat='sites'
    cats[cat].append(r)

for cat,items in cats.items():
    if not items: continue
    print(f'\n  {cat.upper()} ({len(items)})')
    for r in items:
        lang = (r.get('primaryLanguage') or {}).get('name','?') if isinstance(r.get('primaryLanguage'), dict) else '?'
        priv = '🔒' if r.get('isPrivate', True) else '🌍'
        desc = (r.get('description') or '')[:60]
        print(f'  {priv} {r[\"name\"]:<35} {lang:<10} {desc}')
"
", "timeout": 15}
